Re:
Fern Polrrrcar. PRACTTcEs CouvrrssroN
zl28 J Street o Suire 620 r Sacramento, CA g5gl4_232g
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
April 1,2009
Bill Condrashoff
Amador Water Agency Director
11180 Ranchette Drive
Jackson, CA95642
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-072
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections g I 000 through g l0 14. All
statutory refbrences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices commission are contained in Sections l8 I l0 rhrough 1g997 of ritle 2 of the california
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division'6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
Dear Mr. Condrashoff:
This letter responds to your request for follow up advice regarding your duties as
a director with the Amador Water Agency Board ("AWA") under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Please note that our advice is based
solely on provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any,
of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict-of-interesi or Govemment
Code Section 1090. In addition, the Commission will not advise with respect to past
conduct. (Regulation 18329(bX8)(A), copy enclosed.) Therefore, nothin! in this letter
should be construed to evaluate any conduct that may have already taken ilu.", and any
conclusions provided apply only to prospective actions. Also note our advice is based
solely on the facts presented in your request; the Commission does not act as a finder of
fact when it provides advice. (In re ogresby (1975) I Fppc ops. 71.)
QrrEsTroNs
1. May you participate in discussions and decisions involving classification of
water lines as transmission and distribution lines, including lines thaiwill run across your
property?


File No. A-09-072
Page No. 2
2' Are you required to leave the room while a matter for which you have a
conflict of interest is being considered in open session?
3' May you participate in a decision to endorse the designation of a local river as
"wild and scenic," a proposal initiated by the Foothill Conservancy, which is a source of
income to your business?
CONCLUSIONS
l. Because your property is directly involved in the decision, you may onry
participare if there will be nofinancial effect on you ;. t;;, properry, not even one
penny.
-2' The only statutory provision in the Act that requires an official to leave the
room during open session when a matter for which t " o, ,r,e has a conflict of interest is
being considered is section 87105. However, Section 87105 only applies to persons
holding positions that are listed in Section 872Dand would not apply to water board
members' However, local rules may require a member to leave when the member has a
conflict of interest, and we cannot advise on local rules.
3. Because Foothilr conservancy is applying for the designation, you may onry
participate if there will be no financial effect o" nooirril corrervancy, not even one
penny.
FACTS
According to your prior letters:
a
You are a director with the AwA. You were elected in November 200g and took
office on December 5, 200g.
The agency has five elected directors and provides water and wastewater service
within Amador County.
1w+ owns and operates the Canal, a23-mirewater conveyance system. The
canal is open in most sections and is either ri""J;irh;rrii" oriurr earthen.
There are also piped sections of the canal along itr "o,irrl.'The canal transports
untreated water from pacific Gas and Electric's-TabeauJReservoir to the
Agency's Tanner water Treatment Plant. After treatment, the water is distributed
to the communities of Jackson, sutter creek, Ione, AmaJ*'city, Drytown, and
their vicinities. The.canar passes through you, prop"n, *o is arso centered
along one boundary line of your property.
lyA is working on the Amador water System Transmission project
("Transmission Project") to replace the Amador canal. The project consists of an
o
o
a


File No. A-09-072
Page No. 3
a
S-mile pipeline from Tabeaud Reservoir to the Tanner Treatment prant. This
pipeline is complete and does not run thrgugh you, prop"rry. rn. stated goars of
the Transmission project incrude improviniwrr". a"rirl"ry to the Tanner
Treatment facility, improving water qualitv] una uaar"rririg prourems relating to
service to existing customersalong the Canal.
The project also involves dewatering the canal and laying smaller pipeline in the
Canal to continue serving existing niut., users arong the Canar. you own gg acres
of land (one parcel) in Amador c-ounty. your resid-ence is located on this
property. However,.the.smafl diameter pipeline wourd ue instauea in the canal
qttough your property along the path orirri canal. you-ao not use water from the
Canal.
n"* ru.ll,rour 
March 18, 2009, request for folrow up advice, you provided the folrowing
o The AWA will be considering the classification of the new pipeline that will run
t-lgt, your property. This line wil be either u tr-r-irrion line or a
distribution line. The classification of the Iines will determine which customers
will pay the cost of the line. You stated the cosr of the line will u, p"ia uin,or,
of the system customers.
o The Foothill conservancy (the "conseryancy") is a nonprofit organization and has
paid your printing business (owned 50vo by'yo, uiaiciE"by your spouse) $r,227
in the past 12 months. The conservancy hasie", rouuying the AwA to consider
endorsing the designation of a rocal river as .,wild and icenic.,, The AwA cannot
designate a river "wild and scenic" because federal Iaw limits this power to
congress, or the Secretary of the Interior (if the river is a part of a state river
protection system and the state governor applies to the Secretary). (16 uscs g
1273.) 
r --- 'v w"Y v
o d conservancy officiar stated that if the decision is approved they may lose
funding for the purpose of pursuing the design"ati";. "ih. conservancy has total
annual receipts between $100,000 La $t,OO-O,OOO.
AIYALYSIS
ln our prior letters to you we discussed the eight_step standard analysis for
deciding whether an.official has a disqualifying corfl?t oiint"."rt. (Reguration
18700(b)') In those letters we conclud'ed that;., rr; ; ,fittic official,,under rhe Act
who would be making, participating in making, or otheniise using your official position
to influence govemmental decisioni in satisfaction of the first two steps of the analysis.
In this letter you request guidance with respect to three specific decisions:


File No. A-09-072
Page No.4
1. Discussions and decisions involving classification of water lines as
transmission and distribution lines.
2. A decision to endorse the designation of a local river as "wild and scenic.,,
As a follow-up to this earlier analysis you ask whether the Act requires you to
"leave the room when a matter for which you hau" a conflict of interest is being
considered. Section 87105 of the Act provides that certain public officials must
immediately prior to the discussion of an item for which the official has a conflict of
interest: (l) orally identify each type of economic interest involved in the decision:
(2) recuse himself or herself: and (3) leave the room for the duration of the discussion
and./or vote on the item. However, Section 87105 by its express terms only applies to
officials holding positions that are listed in Section A2OO. Section g7200 provides the
following:
"This article is applicable to elected state officers, judges and
commissioners of courts of the judicial branch of government] members of
the Public Utilities Commission, members of the State Energy Resources
conservation and Deveropment commission, members of thi Fair
Political Practices Commission, members of the California Coastal
Commission, members of planning commissions, members of the board of
supervisors, district attorneys, county counsels, county treasurers, and
chief administrative officers of counties, mayors, city managers, city
attorneys, city treasurers, chief administrative officers and members of
city councils of cities, and other public officials who manage public
investments, and to candidates for any of these offices at any ilection.',
_ 
Because your position is not listed in Section 87200, you are not subject to
Section 87105 of the Act.
However, Regulation 18702.1(a)(5) does provide that "[w]hen the determination
not to act [in a decision] occurs because of the ofiicial's financial interest, the official,s
determination may be accompanied by an oral or written disclosure of the financial
interest." Thus, disclosure of the conflict of interest is permissive, but leavinjthe room
is not required. Finally, in a comment to Regul ation ld7O2.l the Commissioi stated:
"Nothing in this section authorizes or prohibits an agency by local rule or custom from
requiring a disqualified member to step down from the dais and/or leave the chambers.,,
Thus, you should be mindful of any local rule that may apply to you.
Step 3. What are your economic interests?
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from certain enumerated economic interests. tn y-ouiprioi l"tt"., we focused on your
economic interest in real property. In this request you also ask a question that implicates


File No. A-09-072
Page No. 5
your mterest in your business and sources.of income to your business.2 section g7103(c)
provides that an official has an economic i11elest i, ury ,ou.ce of income, including
promised income, totaling $500 or more within t2 months prior to the decision. (section
87 103(c); Regulation I g703.3.)
You stated that you are a 50 percent owner of a printing business. pursuant to
Section 82030, because. you are a 5O-percent owner of the business , 50 percent of the
income to the business is treated as income directly to you. you stated that the
conservancy has paid your printing business $1,2;7 initre past 12 months. your 50
percent share of that income would be $613.50. g."urr;ilre conservancy has paid you
$500 or more in \" l? months prior to the decision,,rr"y are considered a source of
income to you under the Act.3
Step 4' Are your economic interests directly or indirectly involved in the
decisions in question?
Real Property: Under Regulation l87o4.2,the general rule is that an official,s
real property is considered direct[, involved i, u gou".i^Lntal decision if, among other
criteria, the following applies:
"(l) The real property in which the official has an interest, or any
part of that real property, is located within 500 feet of the uouna*i!, ro.
the proposed boundaries) of the property that is the subject of the
govemmental decision;,'
Additionally, real property is directly involved in a decision if the decision
involves the construction ol ir i*pto*.n"nts to, streets, water, sewer, storm drainage, or
similar facilities, and the real property in which the official has an interest will receive
new or improved services. lRegulation lg70a.2@)(6).)
As we discussed in our prior letter, because the pipeline will run right across your
property' your real property interest is dtrectry invorvei iithese decisions.-
' 
sources of Inqome: Regulation 18704.1(a) states that a business entity or source
of income is.directly involved iir a decision befoie the official,s agency when that
business entity or source of income, either directry or by an-agent:
"(l) Initiates the proceeding in which the decision will be made by
filing an application, claim, appeaf or similar."qu"r, o.,
2 You also have an economic interest in your printing business under Section g7103(a), (c) and
i?;#lffl'i,i;v<rur 
business is not implicated in the aetisiorira y", irve nor asked us ro anaryze effects
I In addition' you.have a community property interest in one-half of your spouse,s income, so in
effect you will be attributed 75vo of income to ir,.-uu.in"rs. (sera AJ;. Lerrer, No. A-01-217.)


File No. A-09-072
Page No. 6
"(2) Ir alamed party in,_or is the subject of, the proceeding
concerning the decision before the official oi the officiai's ug"n"y."A
person is the subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the issuance,
renewal, approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or other
entitlement to, or contract with, the subject parron.,,
You indicated that the "conseryancy" has been lobbying the AWA to consider
endorsing the designation of a local river ai "wild and scenic." Thus, the conservancy is
the applicant with respect to that decision and is directly involved.
Steps 5 and 6. What is the applicable materiality standard and is a material
financial effect reasonably foreseeable?
Real Property: A conflict of interest may arise only when the reasonably
foreseeable financial effect of a governmental dlcision on a public official,s economic
interest is material. (Regulation 18700(a).) Different standards apply to a"i"r-i*
whether a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on an economic interest will be
material, depending on the nature of the economic interest and whether that interest is
directly or indirectly involved in the agency's decision.
For real property directly involved in a govemmental decision, any financial
effect of the decision, even "one penny," is preJumed to be material. (Regulation
18705'2(a)(l).) This presumptio, -ay be ributted by proof that it is ,ot"r"aronably
foreseeable that the govemmental decision will have a iy-financial effect on the real
property. (lbid.)
Sources of Income: The financial effect on sources of income that are nonprofit
entities is material if there is any reasonably foreseeable financial effect on the source of
income resulting from the decision. (Reguiation 1g705.3(a).)
once a public official identifies his or her relevant economic interests, the official
must evaluate whethqr it^is- reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect on one of those economic interests. An effect is considered ..reasonably
foreseeable" if the effect is "substantially likely." 6"lutaiion 1g706; In re Thorner
(.1975) I FPPC ops. 198.) whether the iinanciat "o"rJq*ces of a governmental
decision are reasonably foreseeable at the time the decision is made il r,ighr/rituarion-
specific' A financial effect need not be a certainty to be considered ."u.onuurf
foreseeable' on the other hand, if an effect is oniy u rn"r. porsibility, it is not'reasonably
foreseeable.
Under your facts, while the ultimate decision to make the local river..wild and
scenic" will have a financial effect on the nonprofit, it does not appear reasonably
foreseeable that the AWA's endorsement, by iiserl wourd have such an effect.
ultimately, however, the determination of whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the
applicable materiality standard will be met for your economic interests is necessarily a


File No. A-09-072
Page No. 7
factual question that is one for the official to make based the facts and circumstances
existing at the time of the decision. (Hensley Advice Letter, No. A-07-l 13; Moock
Advice Letter, No. A-01-l40; o'Harra Advice Letter, No. 4-00-174.)
Steps 7 and 8: The Public Generally and Legally Required participation
Exceptions
An official who otherwise has a conflict of interest in a decision may still
participate under the-"public generally" exception. This exception applies when the
financial effect of a decision on a public offiiial's economic interesti is substantially the
same as the effect on a significant segment of the public. The ,.legally required
participation" rule applies when the official's participation in u gou"-m"nial decision is
legally required. (Secrion 87101; Regulation iSZOgj
You have not suggested that either of these exceptions are applicable to your
situation, therefore we do not address them here.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
W. Wallace
sistant General Counsel
Legal Division
Jwwjgl
Enclosure
By: J

All statutory refbrences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair Political Practices commission are contained in Sections l8 I l0 rhrough 1g997 of ritle 2 of the california Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division'6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. Dear Mr. Condrashoff: This letter responds to your request for follow up advice regarding your duties as a director with the Amador Water Agency Board ("AWA") under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Please note that our advice is based solely on provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict-of-interesi or Govemment Code Section 1090. In addition, the Commission will not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(bX8)(A), copy enclosed.) Therefore, nothin! in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that may have already taken ilu.", and any conclusions provided apply only to prospective actions. Also note our advice is based solely on the facts presented in your request; the Commission does not act as a finder of fact when it provides advice. (In re ogresby (1975) I Fppc ops. 71.) QrrEsTroNs 1. May you participate in discussions and decisions involving classification of water lines as transmission and distribution lines, including lines thaiwill run across your property? File No. A-09-072 Page No. 2 2' Are you required to leave the room while a matter for which you have a conflict of interest is being considered in open session? 3' May you participate in a decision to endorse the designation of a local river as "wild and scenic," a proposal initiated by the Foothill Conservancy, which is a source of income to your business? CONCLUSIONS l. Because your property is directly involved in the decision, you may onry participare if there will be nofinancial effect on you ;. t;;, properry, not even one penny. -2' The only statutory provision in the Act that requires an official to leave the room during open session when a matter for which t " o, ,r,e has a conflict of interest is being considered is section 87105. However, Section 87105 only applies to persons holding positions that are listed in Section 872Dand would not apply to water board members' However, local rules may require a member to leave when the member has a conflict of interest, and we cannot advise on local rules. 3.
